posts.body:
Hi, everyone.
Could not find it in the forums: is it allowed to keep your (up to three) reserved cards face-down ? Or are they supposed to be "public info" all the time ?
We have always played keeping reserved cards face down.  I will double check the rules in my copy.
I believe that they have to be face-down until they are purchased. This is to separate them from the player’s pool so they aren’t “accidentally” used to purchase cards during gameplay.
According to the rules, reserved cards "are kept in hand". We always interpreted this as being hidden from the other players.

geekboy42 wrote:

We have always played keeping reserved cards face down.  I will double check the rules in my copy.

So in my copy, nothing in the rules explicitly states reserved cards must be kept face down. However when it discusses how to reserve a card, it does mention that one method is to reserve from the top of a deck and do not show the other players the card.

(Just following up on what the rules show.)
We keep them face up since it was open information when they were purchased. But there is no justification for that in the rules.

The rules are ambiguous. My spouse and I find it more strategically satisfying to keep them face up. Face down would remove a lot of the most interesting player interaction and jockeying for position, in my view.
We've always interpreted that the reserve cards are face-down.

Oliver is right: the rules states that the card are kept in hand therefor are not public information.

(Side note: it is not fair play to take a visible card from the tableau before all players have seen it)

Strogonoffre wrote:

Hi, everyone.
Could not find it in the forums: is it allowed to keep your (up to three) reserved cards face-down ? Or are they supposed to be "public info" all the time ?

The rules on reserving a card:

Reserve a development card
To reserve a card, a player simply needs to take a face-up development from the middle of the table or (if you’re feeling lucky) draw the first card from one of the three decks (level O; OO; OOO)  without showing it to the other players.  The reserved cards are kept in hand and cannot be discarded.  Players may not have more than three reserved cards in hand, and the only way to get rid of a card is to buy it (see below).  Reserving a card is also the only way to get a gold token (joker).  If there is no gold left, you can still reserve a card, but you won’t get any gold.

The vast majority of games in which players have a "hand of cards" means that those cards are hidden from other players.  Otherwise they would be called something other than a "hand of cards."  If they were meant to be face-up on the table, they would be called something else, such as your "pile of reserved cards."  But the general consensus in the forums seems to be that reserved cards are face-down (in your hand), and your purchased cards are face-up (in your tableau).  If they were *all* kept face-up on the table, there is a much greater chance of mixing up the cards that you have already purchased with the cards that you have reserved but not yet purchased.

The Splendor rules make it clear that the cards you reserve from one of the face-down decks remain hidden.  There are other games where you are also able to draw from either a face-up pile, or a face-down pile, and the drawn cards go into your hidden "hand of cards" (Ticket to Ride springs immediately to mind).  In that game, as well as in Splendor, it would be too much trouble to separate out the Hidden but Trackable Information (HTI).  In Ticket to Ride, it makes little sense to keep a *hidden* hand of cards which you have drawn from the face-down deck, and a separate *visible* hand of cards which you have drawn from the face-up offering.  I feel the same way, to a lesser degree, about the reserve cards in Splendor.

In my games of Splendor, *all* reserved cards go face down.

Also, much more reading on the subject, here:

Are reserved cards hidden?
Do cards reserved blind remain blind?
Hiding your reserved cards?
Hidden or not?
Reserve card: Visible or no?

Also, from this six year old thread (Re: Hidden or not?), we have this:

rkonigsberg wrote:

Hidden reserve cards seems to be the correct play style. I confirmed this with an email to Space Cowboys. /shrug

Cheers!

Thank you, guys !
We will try face-down / hidden reserved cards, it makes sense for all the reasons you stressed. If my group disapproves it, we can always house-rule it otherwise.
